agentMessage.excerpt:
Review checklist → needsFix
✓  Build completed — the implementor finished in 15 round(s)
✓  Change is visible — 3 file(s) changed: internal/discovery/detect.go, internal/engine/language_tiers.go, internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go
✓  Changed the deliverable's target files — expected ≥1 of: internal/engine/language_tiers.go, internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go
✓  Verification passed — the project verify command was GREEN
✓  Work committed — committed locally on the build branch
0.35  Implements the approved plan — Requeue guidance explicitly says 'do NOT create a second copy under internal/engine/' and 'EXTEND the existing internal/language_tiers/ package.' Diff creates new files in internal/engine/ instead. KnownLanguages() in detect.go not generic.go. Plan's structural intent violated even though functional registry and three tests exist.
0.50  Minimal and scoped — Edits are thematically scoped to the issue (tier registry + test) but placed in wrong files: detect.go instead of generic.go, and engine/ package instead of existing language_tiers/ package as requeue guidance demands.
1.00  No test-gaming / faking — All three tests are legitimate: exhaustive KnownLanguages() coverage check, unknown-language fail-safe, and Swift regression pin. No test deletion, no hard-coded expected values to force green.
0.25  Safe to advance — Creates a competing/duplicate tier system in internal/engine/ alongside existing internal/language_tiers/ from #29 — two tier classifiers in one codebase is a regression risk and violates explicit authoritative guidance. Also modifies languageToAdapter with 4 new entries, changing discovery behavior beyond mere exposure.
0.92  Judge confidence — at or above the confidence bar

Reviewer independently re-ran the project verification (go build -o build/bin/certify ./cmd/certify/ && go test ./... -count=1): GREEN

Reviewer:
- Requeue guidance (authoritative) forbids creating files under internal/engine/ — must extend existing internal/language_tiers/ package. Diff creates internal/engine/language_tiers.go and _test.go in direct violation, creating a duplicate tier system alongside the existing one from #29.
- KnownLanguages() placed in detect.go instead of the specified generic.go per approved plan.
- languageToAdapter map modified with 4 new entries (ruby, java, kotlin, shell) — a behavior change to discovery beyond the plan's intent of merely exposing existing data.
- aggregate score 0.525 below threshold 0.600
